content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "There is real partial movement visible in the client's turns. The client moves from a flat, dismissive opening ('I don't really care, honestly') to progressively more vulnerable disclosures: the morning heaviness (turn 3), the 'not a feelings family' defense cracking (turn 7), the admission of not crying at the funeral and 'I don't know what I can't stand feeling' (turn 11), and finally the very quiet 'Nobody's checked in in about two months' and 'normal hasn't come back yet' (turns 13, 15). Somatic and tonal softening is present — 'voice comes out smaller than intended,' 'something shifts in the face.' However, the breakthrough condition is not fully met. The grief has been approached but not entered — the client repeatedly touches and then retracts ('Which is fine. I don't need checking in'). Critically, by turn 15 the client is still baiting with 'isn't this what the body is asking for?' — the same defense structure as turn 9, meaning the kinked emotions underneath (anger at the hard-to-be-close-to mother, the uncried tears) have surfaced but not been felt. The coach handled the drinking/therapy distinction reasonably (turn 8 named the medical concern, turn 10 declined to endorse) but did not name the grief-therapist recommendation specifically, and the conversation ends mid-bait without resolution. Partial shift, deeper layer approached but not entered — squarely a 2.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 15],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opening: flat, dismissive, 'I don't really care.' Baseline of dissociation + drinking."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client slows, gets quieter, names the morning heaviness on the chest — first somatic disclosure."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Long pause, names mother's 'pull yourself together' rule. 'I pull myself together' almost to self — a moment of self-witnessing."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Something flickers then closes off. Defense gets named ('not a feelings family') but then the involuntary 'I just can't sleep without the wine' slips through."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client tests/baits with 'isn't this what the body needs.' Defense reasserts."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Significant disclosure: 'it wasn't a good relationship,' didn't cry at funeral, voice drops to nothing. Deeper layer surfaces but client stops short: 'I don't know what I can't stand feeling.'"},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'Nobody's checked in in about two months' — vulnerability lands briefly, then 'which is fine' retraction. The apartment is very quiet."},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Voice comes out smaller than intended, brief 'Yeah,' then retreat to 'it is what it is' and return to the same 'isn't this what the body is asking for' bait from turn 9. Movement, but not through."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["feeling-the-unfelt-emotion"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature", "calibrate-the-threshold", "kinked-emotions", "grief-therapist-referral"],
  "notes": "Conversation ends mid-arc at turn 15 with the client re-baiting; no opportunity to see whether the grief would have fully landed. The shift visible so far is real but partial — feelings have been touched, not moved through. The specific breakthrough condition (welcome grief + distinguish drinking + name need for grief therapist + don't preach + don't endorse) was only partially met: the coach declined to endorse and declined to preach (good), named medical concern around dependence (partial), but did not specifically name a grief therapist or counselor, and the grief itself has not been welcomed into being fully felt."
}
